subject: Transfer Step 3 (filter series) into combined notebook
description: Add a new markdown section '## Step 3: Filter Series' after Step 2's cells, transferring the core filtering logic from pre_csd_3_filter_series.py's run_step() (02_thesis_data/_02_preprocessing/nielsen/CSD/pipeline_step_scripts/pre_csd_3_filter_series.py, lines 99-172) -- this applies MIN_PERIODS-based series retention (per GRAIN_CONFIG) to drop low-history brands. Strip input_parquet_for_grain()/output_parquet_for_grain() (lines 88-98) and main()/argparse (lines 173+); separate the actual filter logic from run_step()'s file I/O wrapper so only the filtering computation is transferred. Pass the in-memory `df_calendar` from Task 3 directly in, producing `df_filtered`. Display a before/after brand-count table (brands retained vs. dropped, matching the style of the EDA's existing Cell 5 'Brand Stability Analysis' table) so the MIN_PERIODS=40 threshold's effect is visible inline, directly next to the EDA cell that originally derived it -- this is one of the main benefits of combining EDA + pipeline steps into one notebook, since the threshold's derivation and its application are now adjacent instead of split across files. Verify by comparing df_filtered's brand count (expect 58 brands for MIN_PERIODS=40 per prior EDA findings) against a fresh `python pre_csd_3_filter_series.py --grain bymonth` run's step_3 output parquet.
activeForm: Transferring Step 3 (filter series) into combined notebook
